title: rbac — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by RbacServiceProvider::boot() so any FormRequest / Data class can validate role + permission writes. All rules can be used inline via the string signature OR as class instances for composition.

rules.name: valid_role_name
rules.description: Enforces role naming conventions: snake_case, max 128 chars, no whitespace, no reserved prefixes (`system.`, `_`), no ASCII-controlled characters. Applied on CreateRoleAction + UpdateRoleAction.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid role name (snake_case, no reserved prefixes).

rules.name: valid_permission_name
rules.description: Enforces permission naming: dot-notation (`resource.action.scope`), max 128 chars, ends with a suffix from config('rbac.permissions.allowed_suffixes'). Refuses names starting with any reserved_prefix. Enforced only when config('rbac.permissions.require_scope_suffix')=true (default).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be dot-notated with a valid scope suffix (e.g. .own, .tenant, .branch).

rules.name: permission_belongs_to_role_guard
rules.description: Cross-check on write. Enforces role.guard_name === permission.guard_name. Applied on AttachPermissionToRoleAction. Distinct from the observer's GuardMismatch enforcement — this rule surfaces the error inline in the FormRequest so the client sees a validation error, not a 500.

params.name: role_id
params.description: ULID of the target role (fetched from the URL segment).

rules.default_message: The permission's guard does not match the role's guard.

rules.name: permission_belongs_to_role_application
rules.description: Same shape as permission_belongs_to_role_guard, for the application_id column. Both non-null => must match. Either NULL => allowed (platform-scoped bindings).
rules.default_message: The permission's application does not match the role's application.

rules.name: role_assignable_by_actor
rules.description: The privilege-escalation guard. A role can only be assigned by an actor whose own ResolvedPermissionSet is a SUPERSET of the target role's permission set. This prevents an admin from creating a custom role with permissions they lack + then assigning it to themselves. Enforced by AssignRoleToPrincipalAction.
rules.default_message: You cannot assign a role holding permissions you do not have.

rules.name: role_not_system
rules.description: Refuses mutations targeting a role where is_system=true. Applied to UpdateRoleAction + DeleteRoleAction (tenant-plane only).

rules.name: principal_belongs_to_current_application
rules.description: For assign/revoke routes on principal-role pivots. Refuses assignments where principal.application_id does NOT match the current request's X-Application-Id. Prevents cross-Application role bleed via the API.
rules.default_message: The target principal does not belong to the current Application.

usage_from_form_request: The AssignRoleToPrincipalAction's Data class composes these rules automatically. Most callers never write these rules manually. This file is the canonical registry for the underlying rule implementations.
